content.claim: Cross-national quasi-experimental evidence (Hanushek & Woessmann) and a 2021 meta-analysis of 75 countries found that early tracking increases educational inequality and social achievement gaps, with unclear or negative effects on mean performance
content.context: Fresh live research through the Finnish system reform lens
limitations: Effect sizes and mechanisms remain debated across studies using different data and domains
Hungary's age-10/12 timing parameter is an outlier, making direct comparisons to studies of age-15/16 tracking systems uncertain
Meta-analysis citation appears truncated in source notes, limiting full assessment of methodology
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
